target_title: feat(companion): add Biome for linting and formatting
target_body:
## What does this PR do?

Replaces ESLint and Prettier with [Biome](https://biomejs.dev/) for linting and formatting in the companion app. Biome is a fast, all-in-one toolchain that handles both linting and formatting with a single configuration file.

**Changes:**
- Added `biome.json` configuration with formatting settings matching previous Prettier config (2 spaces, double quotes, semicolons, trailing commas ES5, line width 100)
- Added Biome scripts to package.json: `lint`, `format`, `format:check`, `check`, `check:ci`
- Updated `lint-staged` to use `biome check --write --error-on-warnings` for pre-commit (runs both formatting AND linting, fails on warnings)
- Removed ESLint and Prettier dependencies (`eslint`, `eslint-config-expo`, `eslint-config-prettier`, `eslint-plugin-prettier`, `prettier`, `prettier-plugin-tailwindcss`)
- Deleted `.prettierrc.js` configuration file
- Applied Biome formatting and safe lint fixes to all companion files

**Note:** Tailwind CSS class sorting is no longer automatically applied since Biome doesn't have a Tailwind plugin. This was a conscious tradeoff for simpler tooling.

## Updates since last revision

**Enabled `noUnusedVariables` rule** (4 violations fixed):
- `AuthTokenProvider.tsx`: Prefixed `isWebSession` with underscore (destructured from useAuth but not used)
- `calcom.ts`: Prefixed `isOrganizer`, `isHost`, `isAttendee` with underscore (only `isParticipating` is used for filtering)

**Previously enabled lint rules:**
- `noUnusedFun
source_title: fix(companion): address security vulnerabilities from rnsec report
source_body:
## What does this PR do?

Addresses security vulnerabilities identified by rnsec security scanning tool in the companion (Expo/React Native) app and browser extension. This PR implements security hardening to prevent sensitive data exposure in production logs, adds request timeout protection, and adds safe JSON parsing.

**Key changes:**
- Add `safeLogger.ts` utility with `safeLogError`, `safeLogWarn`, `safeLogInfo` functions that only log in `__DEV__` mode
- Add `fetchWithTimeout` utility to prevent requests from hanging indefinitely (30s default timeout)
- Replace all `console.error/warn/log` calls with safe logging functions across 15+ files
- Use generic error messages in user-facing alerts instead of exposing raw `error.message` details
- Add safe JSON parsing functions with structure validation to prevent prototype pollution

**Security issues addressed:**
- SENSITIVE_DATA_IN_ERROR_MESSAGES - Error objects no longer logged in production
- NO_REQUEST_TIMEOUT - All fetch calls now have 30-second timeout
- SENSITIVE_LOGGING - Debug logs wrapped with `__DEV__` checks
- INSECURE_DESERIALIZATION - JSON.parse calls now use validation functions with prototype chain checks
- IOS_DATA_PROTECTION_MISSING - Added NSFileProtectionComplete entitlement
- ANDROID_CLEARTEXT_ENABLED - Disabled cleartext traffic in app.json

### Updates since last revision

Enhanced JSON parsing security and added platform security configurations:

- **services/calcom.ts & background/index.ts**: Enhanced

target_title: feat(ui): Add unified ui prop with RSC support via conditional exports
target_body:
## Summary

Refactors the UI loading architecture so users only interact with an opaque `ui` prop from `@clerk/ui`. Each SDK internally decides whether to use the bundled UI constructor or load from CDN.

**Key addition:** Added `react-server` conditional export so the `ui` prop works directly in Next.js App Router server components without requiring a client wrapper.

### Key Changes

- **Public API**: Users pass `ui={ui}` - the object is opaque and branded
- **Removed `clerkUiCtor`** from public `IsomorphicClerkOptions` (kept internally as `ui.ClerkUI` for SDK use)
- **Default behavior**: Bundled UI is used when available (`ui.ClerkUI`)
- **React Server Components support**: Added `react-server` conditional export that provides a server-safe marker
- **Version pinning**: CDN loading uses `ui.version` to pin the UI version

### How It Works

The `@clerk/ui` package uses conditional exports:

```json
".": {
  "react-server": "./dist/server.js",  // Server-safe marker (no ClerkUI)
  "default": "./dist/index.js"          // Full export (with ClerkUI)
}
```

| Context | Export | Contains |
|---------|--------|----------|
| Next.js RSC | `server.js` | `{ __brand, version }` |
| Client / Other SDKs | `index.js` | `{ __brand, version, ClerkUI }` |

When IsomorphicClerk receives the `ui` prop:
1. If `ui.ClerkUI` exists → use directly (bundled)
2. If `ui.__brand === '__clerkUI'` but no `ClerkUI` → dynamic import from `@clerk/ui/entry`
3. Otherwise → load from CDN

### SDK Behavior

|
source_title: fix(clerk-js): Restore backwards compat for legacy clerkUICtor option
source_body:
## Summary
- Restores backwards compatibility for the legacy `clerkUICtor` / `clerkUiCtor` options that were removed in #7664
- The `#initOptions` method now detects legacy option names and converts them to the new `ui.ClerkUI` format
- Adds test coverage for both legacy option names

## Context
The `ui` prop PR (#7664) changed the clerk-js options API from `clerkUICtor` (top-level) to `ui: { ClerkUI }` (nested) and removed the backwards compat shim. Consumers still on older SDK versions (e.g. the dashboard) pass `clerkUICtor`, which the new clerk-js ignores — causing `assertComponentsReady` to throw "Clerk was not loaded with Ui components" when mounting any component.

Failing dashboard smoke tests: https://github.com/clerk/dashboard/actions/runs/21809499309

## Test plan
- [x] Existing `ui.ClerkUI` test still passes
- [x] New test: legacy `clerkUICtor` option initializes ClerkUI
- [x] New test: legacy `clerkUiCtor` option initializes ClerkUI
- [x] `pnpm build --filter @clerk/clerk-js` passes
- [x] All 97 clerk.test.ts tests pass

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Bug Fixes**
  * Restored backwards compatibility for legacy Clerk UI constructor options so older option names continue to work with the updated UI initialization.

* **Tests**
  * Added tests verifying legacy UI constructor options invoke the expected initialization path.

* **Documentation**
  * Added a changeset entry documenting the patch-l

source_body:
Implements the internal `getValidStdio` function used by Node.js's child_process module to validate stdio configurations. This function:

  - Validates stdio is a string ('ignore', 'pipe', 'inherit', 'overlapped') or array
  - Expands arrays to at least 3 elements
  - Converts stream objects (like process.stdin) to fd descriptors
  - Tracks IPC channel positions
  - Throws appropriate errors for invalid configurations

 Also, enables test-child-process-validate-stdio.js in node_compat.

target_body:
## What does this PR do?

Moves 17 files out of `packages/features/` into `apps/web/modules/` and reorganizes 2 files within `packages/features/` to reduce cross-package tRPC coupling.

### Impact

- **7 `@calcom/trpc` imports removed from `packages/features/`** — the moved files (`useSegments`, `useSchedule`, `useApiV2AvailableSlots`, `BookerLayoutSelector`, schedule `types`, `FeedbackDialog`, `Segment`) all depended on `@calcom/trpc` and now live in `apps/web/` where tRPC usage belongs

### What moved where

| From | To |
|---|---|
| `packages/features/data-table/hooks/useSegments.ts` | `apps/web/modules/data-table/hooks/` |
| `packages/features/schedules/lib/use-schedule/*` (5 files) | `apps/web/modules/schedules/hooks/` + `lib/` |
| `packages/features/schedules/lib/use-schedule/useTimesForSchedule.ts` | `packages/features/schedules/hooks/` (reorganized, inlined type) |
| `packages/features/feature-opt-in/components/*` (5 files) | `apps/web/modules/feature-opt-in/components/` |
| `packages/features/feedback/components/FeedbackDialog.tsx` | `apps/web/modules/feature-opt-in/components/` |
| `packages/features/settings/BookerLayoutSelector.tsx` | `apps/web/modules/settings/components/` |
| `packages/features/Segment.tsx` + test | `apps/web/modules/event-types/components/` |

No functional changes — only file moves and import path updates.

source_title: fix: Atom build failing due to dependency on @calcom/web
source_body: Atom build fails when it relies on web components that use the alias `"~/..."`. Atoms shouldn't rely on web components in the first place, but until then this is a clean fix.

target_title: ci(repo): Re-enable Turbo remote cache for all CI jobs
target_body:
## Summary

- Re-enables Turbo remote cache (`remote:rw` with signature verification) for all CI workflow jobs: `pre-checks`, `build-packages`, `static-analysis`, `unit-tests`, `integration-tests`, and `pkg-pr-new`
- Remote caching was temporarily disabled in June 2023 and never re-enabled — this restores it so that unchanged packages can skip builds/tests/lints across jobs via shared cache hits
- The `canary-release` and `snapshot-release` jobs in `release.yml` already had remote caching enabled; this brings CI into parity

## Notes

- Verify that `TURBO_TOKEN` and `TURBO_TEAM` secrets/variables are still valid — they've been unused in CI since 2023
- The init-blacksmith action defaults `turbo-cache` to `remote:rw` and `turbo-enabled` to `true`, so providing the credentials is sufficient to activate remote caching
- Cache signing is enabled via `turbo-signature` to prevent cache poisoning

## Test plan

- [ ] Verify `TURBO_TOKEN` secret and `TURBO_TEAM` variable are still valid in repo settings
- [ ] Monitor first CI run for cache miss/hit behavior in build-packages logs
- [ ] Confirm downstream jobs (static-analysis, unit-tests) get cache hits for unchanged packages

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Chores**
  * Re-enabled caching and build-optimization settings in the CI workflow.
  * Added runtime validation for CI inputs to prevent misconfiguration and fail early when required values are missing.
  *
source_title: fix(repo): disable turbo remote cache for release workflow

- Disables turbo remote cache for the `release` and `cache-for-alternate-node-versions` jobs in the release workflow

target_title: fix(clerk-js): Update error handling and adjust Web3WalletMenu logic with verification status
target_body:
## Description

<!--
  Thanks for contributing to Clerk. Make sure to read the contributing guide at https://github.com/clerk/javascript/blob/main/docs/CONTRIBUTING.md before opening a PR!

  **Please create a feature request before starting work on any significant change.**

  Write a brief description of the changes introduced in this PR.
  Include screenshots/videos if they help convey the change.

  Also explain how one can test the change.
-->

Currently, if a C2 starts to add their Web3 wallet, but rejects the request to sign the message (e.g. closes the browser window pop up to sign the message), the identifier is still added but marked unverified and still allows users to "Set primary" identifier, which results in a 422 error.

This hides this option from the action menu and only lets the user remove the Web3 connection from their account in order to request to add the connection again and complete the full verification flow.

<!-- Fixes #(issue number) -->

[USER-4238](https://linear.app/clerk/issue/USER-4238/fix-userprofile-web3walletmenu-actions-to-remove-set-primary-when)

## Checklist

- [X] `pnpm test` runs as expected.
- [X] `pnpm build` runs as expected.
- [ ] (If applicable) [JSDoc comments](https://jsdoc.app/about-getting-started.html) have been added or updated for any package exports
- [ ] (If applicable) [Documentation](https://github.com/clerk/clerk-docs) has been updated

## Type of change

- [X] 🐛 Bug fix
- [ ] 🌟 New feat

target_body:
## Description

Refactor endpoint resolution logic so that `EndpointReferenceExpression.GetValueAsync()` properly resolves based on the provided context.  This removes some of the inconsistencies that came about from `ExpressionResolver` resolving the endpoints in it's own special way as `GetValueAsync()` handles the same contextual awareness.

To make this work, DCP now always allocates a `DefaultAspireContainerNetwork` endpoint when a container is created alongside the existing `LocalhostNetwork` endpoint.

```cs
var ctx = new ValueProviderContext
{
    Caller = nginx1.Resource,
};
var url = await nginx1.GetEndpoint("http").GetValueAsync(ctx);
Console.WriteLine(url);
```

Fixes #7153

## Checklist

- Is this feature complete?
  - [X] No. Follow-up changes expected - update any references that previously hardcoded `resoruce.name` that can now use the contextual resolution api 
- Are you including unit tests for the changes and scenario tests if relevant?
  - [X] Yes
- Did you add public API?
  - [X] No
- Does the change make any security assumptions or guarantees?
  - [X] No
- Does the change require an update in our Aspire docs?
  - [X] Yes - https://github.com/microsoft/aspire.dev/issues/366
source_body:
While working on local app run and container tunnel improvements, I discovered to my dismay that the AllocatedEndpoint management API (specifically, `NetworkEndpointSnapshotList.TryAdd()` is quite broken. It will not properly reuse existing snapshots. The result is that it is not possible to properly wait for an AllocatedEndpoint to appear in the system. 

The fix proposed here is to add a better API (namely upsert + asynchronous, cancellable get) and obsolete the old API.

target_title: feat: Migrate icon system to Lucide React for improved consistency and performance
target_body:
### Summary
This PR migrates Cal.com's icon system from string-based props to direct Lucide React components, improving type safety, consistency, and performance.
Changes Made
### Auth & Onboarding (4 files)
- apps/web/modules/auth/verify-view.tsx: Fixed MailOpenIcon naming conflict
- apps/web/modules/onboarding/components/InviteOptions.tsx: Updated GoogleIcon usage
- apps/web/modules/onboarding/teams/invite/csv-upload-modal.tsx: Fixed FileTextIcon usage
- apps/web/modules/onboarding/organization/invite/csv-upload-modal.tsx: Fixed FileTextIcon usage
### Bookings (2 files)  
- apps/web/components/booking/BookingListItem.tsx: Updated clipboard, eye, and circle-check icons
- apps/web/components/booking/actions/bookingActions.ts: Migrated all action icons to LucideIcon components
### Settings (8 files)
- apps/web/modules/settings/my-account/profile-view.tsx: Fixed InfoIcon sizing
- apps/web/modules/settings/ee/teams/components/TeamsListing.tsx: Fixed InfoIcon sizing
- packages/app-store/routing-forms/components/_components/TeamMemberSelect.tsx: Fixed InfoIcon sizing
- packages/features/insights/components/booking/TimezoneBadge.tsx: Fixed InfoIcon sizing
- packages/ui/components/alert/Alert.tsx: Fixed invalid h4 w-4 classes
- apps/web/modules/settings/my-account/components/DynamicLink.tsx: Updated CheckIcon/CopyIcon usage
- apps/web/app/(use-page-wrapper)/settings/(settings-layout)/SettingsLayoutAppDirClient.tsx: Fixed VerticalTabItem icon
- packages/features/ee
source_title: fix: Changed Delete My Account button color from primary to destructive
source_body: ## What does this PR do? This pr change the Delete my account button color inside Delete Account dialogue box from primary to destructive - Fixes #26325 - Fixes [CAL-7000](https://linear.app/calcom/issue/CAL-7000/delete-my-account-button-color-not-correct) #### Image Demo Before: After: ## Mandatory Tasks (DO NOT REMOVE) - [x] I have self-reviewed the code (A decent size PR without self-review might be rejected). - [ ] I have updated the developer docs in /docs if this PR makes changes that would require a [documentation change](https://cal.com/docs). If N/A, write N/A here and check the checkbox. - [x] I confirm automated tests are in place that prove my fix is effective or that my feature works. ## How should this be tested? 1. Go to settings/my-account/profile 2. Click Delete account in the Danger zone 3. Look at the Delete my account button. 4. It's color is now destructive.
